title: Physical Sciences
description: DBE Physical Sciences for Grade 11 deepens understanding of physical and chemical phenomena through scientific inquiry. The curriculum emphasizes the application of scientific models, theories, and laws to explain and predict events in the physical environment. It equips learners with analytical and problem-solving skills, fostering a comprehensive understanding of science's role in technology, society, and the environment.

topics.title: Vectors in Two Dimensions
topics.description: Focuses on determining resultant vectors and resolving vectors into perpendicular components in a two-dimensional Cartesian plane.

topics.title: Newton's Laws and Application
topics.description: Covers various types of forces, drawing force and free-body diagrams, and applying Newton's three laws of motion to equilibrium and non-equilibrium problems.

topics.title: Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation
topics.description: Explores the gravitational force between masses, calculating weight and acceleration due to gravity, and differentiating between mass and weight.

topics.title: Molecular Structure
topics.description: Delves into chemical bonding types, molecular shapes using VSEPR, understanding bond polarity via electronegativity, and the relationship between bond energy and length.

topics.title: Intermolecular Forces
topics.description: Examines various intermolecular forces and their influence on the physical properties of substances like melting points, boiling points, and density.

topics.title: The Chemistry of Water
topics.description: Investigates the unique properties of water, including its polar nature and hydrogen bonding, and how these affect its three phases and moderating effects on Earth's climate.

topics.title: Geometrical Optics: Refraction
topics.description: Covers the principles of light refraction, Snell's Law, critical angles, total internal reflection, and their application in optical fibers.

topics.title: 2D and 3D Wavefronts: Diffraction
topics.description: Explains wave diffraction, Huygen's principle, and how it demonstrates the wave nature of light, including the factors affecting the degree of diffraction.

topics.title: Ideal Gases and Thermal Properties
topics.description: Explores the behavior of ideal gases based on the kinetic molecular theory, applies gas laws, and relates temperature and pressure to molecular kinetic energy.

topics.title: Stoichiometry
topics.description: Deals with quantitative chemical calculations involving molar volume of gases, concentration, limiting reagents, percent yield, and determining chemical formulas from composition.

topics.title: Electrostatics
topics.description: Focuses on calculating electrostatic forces using Coulomb's Law and understanding electric fields and how to represent them with field lines.

topics.title: Electromagnetism
topics.description: Investigates magnetic fields generated by currents using the Right Hand Rule, explores Faraday's Law of induction and magnetic flux, and discusses the environmental impact of electrical cables.

topics.title: Electric Circuits
topics.description: Covers Ohm's Law, analyzing series and parallel circuits, calculating electrical power and energy, and understanding the concept of kilowatt-hour and electricity costs.

topics.title: Energy and Chemical Change
topics.description: Explores energy changes in chemical reactions, distinguishing between exothermic and endothermic processes, relating them to bond energy, and understanding activation energy.

topics.title: Types of Reactions: Acids and Bases
topics.description: Introduces acid-base theories, identifies conjugate pairs and common acids/bases, explains neutralization, indicators, pH scale, and distinguishes between strong/weak and concentrated/dilute solutions.

topics.title: Exploiting the Lithosphere
topics.description: Examines the Earth's lithosphere as a source of minerals, discusses mining methods, and analyzes the historical and environmental impact of mineral processing.

topics.title: Types of Reactions: Redox Reactions
topics.description: Defines oxidation numbers, identifies redox reactions as electron transfer processes, and teaches how to balance redox equations using the ion-electron method.
